finding_results.notes: Two targeted lines revised. Line 198: replaced aphoristic 'Everybody stares at the body because that's where the benchmark lives' with experience-grounded 'Nobody scores it, but that's where I've seen things slip' — anchored in Suki's personal work practice rather than a composed universal claim, with a concrete example ('can I reformat that for you') instead of the 'body' metaphor. Line 402: replaced the composed metaphor 'reached past the box' with evidence-bound 'inferred where you are' — literal, pointing at what the model did on screen rather than packaging the insight into a quotable formulation. Lines ~292-296 (snapshot targets) were already revised in a prior pass, replacing 'The body is for the benchmark. The extra sentence is where product instincts leak' and 'Not break. Slip, maybe' with procedural, work-practice dialogue. Line 390 ('That's what makes it annoying') retained as the single allowed compact analytical line for Exchange 3 per the acceptance test; it is situation-specific (depends on the evidence being discussed) rather than a freestanding maxim. Each of the three anchored exchanges now has at most one maxim-like line; no two can be lifted as stand-alone maxims without the surrounding scene.

summary: Roughened Suki's two remaining composed theory-lines (line 198 and line 402) to depend on immediate evidence and personal work experience rather than delivering liftable aphorisms. Exchange 1 now has zero maxim-like lines; Exchange 2 was already fixed in a prior pass; Exchange 3 retains one borderline analytical line (390) within the acceptance-test limit. All revisions preserve Suki's sharp, fast idiolect while grounding her observations in screen-pointing and evaluator practice rather than writerly mouthpiece formulations.
